title: AI models collapse when trained on recursively generated data
difference_from_our_work: Defines collapse and tail loss but does not allocate a fixed human-token budget across generation and mode.

title: Self-Consuming Generative Models Go MAD
difference_from_our_work: Closest joint ingredients, but the real-data supply is a per-generation fraction rather than one lifetime budget and modes are not monitored human strata.

title: Is Model Collapse Inevitable? Breaking the Curse of Recursion by Accumulating Real and Synthetic Data
models: GPT-2, Llama2, diffusion, VAE, linear regression
difference_from_our_work: Studies accumulation, not scarce-token allocation; spending time and target mode are not decision variables.

title: Large Language Models Suffer From Their Own Output: An Analysis of the Self-Consuming Training Loop
difference_from_our_work: Varies generated-data ratios and fresh data but does not impose or optimize a lifetime human budget.

title: A Tale of Tails: Model Collapse as a Change of Scaling Laws
difference_from_our_work: Characterizes tail-dependent scaling and mixing, not feedback allocation over a matched lifetime budget.

title: Model Collapse Demystified: The Case of Regression
difference_from_our_work: Mitigation changes regularization rather than selecting scarce human examples across time and modes.

title: How Bad is Training on Synthetic Data? A Statistical Analysis of Language Model Collapse
difference_from_our_work: Provides a safe mixture threshold, not an allocation policy for a fixed lifetime stock of human tokens.

title: Self-Correcting Self-Consuming Loops for Generative Model Training
difference_from_our_work: Injects external correction rather than allocating a counted human-origin token budget.

title: Collapse or Thrive: Perils and Promises of Synthetic Data in a Self-Generating World

difference_from_our_work: Fixed-size accumulated sampling is close on budget control, but human-token lifetime use and mode targeting are absent.

title: Rate of Model Collapse in Recursive Training
difference_from_our_work: Quantifies extinction time but does not choose when or where human anchors are spent.

title: Machine-generated text detection prevents language model collapse
difference_from_our_work: Uses importance resampling, but no fixed lifetime human-token constraint and no explicit allocation among human modes.

title: Recursive Training Loops in LLMs: How training data properties modulate distribution shift in generated data?
targets_modes: dataset properties and bias tracked, not selected
difference_from_our_work: Shows monitoring target matters, but does not introduce a rescue budget or selection policy.

title: How to Synthesize Text Data without Model Collapse?
recursive_setting: partly; recursive motivation plus multiple training regimes
fixed_lifetime_budget: per-run token budgets, not lifetime recursive budget

title: Demystifying Synthetic Data in LLM Pre-training: A Systematic Study of Scaling Laws, Benefits, and Pitfalls
difference_from_our_work: Strong evidence on ratio-by-budget interactions, but no recursive chain or adaptive lifetime allocation.

title: Analyzing and Mitigating Model Collapse in Reflow Methods
difference_from_our_work: Guarantees fixed-fraction real augmentation, not a finite lifetime stock with discretionary timing.

title: Escaping Model Collapse via Synthetic Data Verification: Near-term Improvements and Long-term Convergence

difference_from_our_work: Targets bias through rejection sampling but not a fixed human-rescue budget or matched time-mode baselines.

allocates_across_time: yes, offline or online stage schedule

difference_from_our_work: Optimizes mode/domain proportions but not timing or recursive human rescue.

difference_from_our_work: Provides a strong selection-only baseline but no recursive schedule or lifetime human allocation.
